thesis: Adobe is down ~50% from highs and trading near 52-week lows, yet the business continues to grow double-digits with best-in-class margins and cash flow. The primary uncertainty is the timeline and magnitude of AI monetization via Firefly, but Adobe's 'brand-safe' AI positioning and entrenched creative ecosystem provide defensive moats. At ~$193, the risk/reward is skewed positively: even modest multiple expansion could drive significant returns, while the bear case is largely priced in.

financials.net_income: ~$4.12B GAAP EPS ($5.72 non-GAAP EPS in Q2)

drivers: Firefly generative AI monetization accelerates, driving higher-tier subscription upgrades and $1B+ incremental ARR by FY2027
Forward P/E re-rates from ~8x toward 11–15x as AI fears subside and growth proves durable
Digital Experience sustains 17–20% growth as enterprise marketing spend recovers
Non-GAAP operating margins expand toward 50% via subscription scale and AI-driven efficiency
$25B buyback program retires 15–20% of shares at depressed prices, amplifying EPS growth

assumptions: Steady 10–13% revenue growth with AI adoption in line with guidance
Firefly contributes modestly to ARPU but does not transform growth trajectory
Forward P/E expands modestly from ~8x to 10–11x as sentiment stabilizes
Digital Experience grows at mid-teens, offsetting any Creative Cloud deceleration
Buybacks and margin stability support EPS growth in the low-teens

risks: Open-source AI tools (Midjourney, Stable Diffusion, Sora) erode Adobe's creative monopoly and increase churn
Enterprise spending slowdown decelerates Digital Experience growth to high single digits
Heavy AI R&D and GPU infrastructure investment compress operating margins
Market maintains P/E at 8–10x, capping upside and leaving the stock range-bound
AI copyright and IP regulations impose compliance costs and limit feature rollout

catalysts.event: Adobe MAX — new Firefly features, AI product announcements, creative workflow updates
Q3 FY2026 Earnings — Digital Experience momentum, Firefly adoption metrics, FY guidance
Q4 FY2026 / Full-Year Results — revenue/EPS validation, FY2027 guidance, AI monetization disclosure
Firefly paid tier adoption data — subscriber upgrades, ARPU impact, enterprise licensing deals
Share buyback execution — consistent capital returns provide downside support
AI model updates — Firefly video generation, 3D content, real-time collaboration features

key_risks: AI disruption of creative tools by open-source and competing generative models
Enterprise marketing technology spending slowdown affecting Digital Experience growth
Delayed monetization of Firefly generative AI features
Regulatory and copyright risks around AI training data and output licensing
Margin compression from elevated AI R&D and GPU infrastructure investment
Talent competition in AI/ML research and engineering
